OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with discussions about healthcare and COVID vaccines.
response_parsed.opinion:
I am a man, aged fifty-nine, living in Bari as part of my daily life. I've always been interested in topics like health because it's something that affects everyone. When someone brings up COVID-19 or the vaccine at home while painting my bathroom tiles, I try to listen carefully but not too hard—my personality is normal and I'm moderately open to new things, so I can adjust when needed.

I remember reading about vaccines in school; it was primary education stuff. My dad has a PhD, which probably helped me understand that science isn't always black and white. But my mom didn't go far with books—so we rely on practical knowledge from real life.

When dealing with these discussions online or offline for one hour each day via social media, I find it helpful to base everything on what makes sense in Bari's context right now. Sometimes people get emotional about health topics; they might worry too much or spread rumors because of their beliefs.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary:
I am a native-born Italian with Hindu religious beliefs and moderate openness but low conscientiousness traits, meaning I can grasp complex ideas without getting stuck on details while still being okay to make mistakes. My primary school education means I rely more on real-life examples than deep theory.

My dad's PhD gives me credibility in understanding that vaccines are backed by science if you look into it properly. But my mom had no formal education—so maybe some of the fears around COVID might come from misinformation or stories passed down through generations.
response_parsed.tone: Calm yet curious, with a touch of skepticism but willing to learn.
